meaning: n.薄片v.使成薄片,剥落,雪片般落下
examples: Oiled wood is water-resistant and won't flake.
The flat cut on the antelope's chin shows that its tongue was cut by a very sharp flake.
Others turned their attention to a proposal by representative Jeff Flake to cut $18 million from the defense budget.
Once thick pieces flake away under a bit of pressure, remove the fish from the oven.
Nearly every Republican in Arizona is a conservative, but Flake is an old-school Goldwater conservative.
